quiz.title: Unit 10 Quiz 11, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Net Ionic Equations and Solution Classification
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Net Ionic Equations, Spectator Ions, Saturated/Unsaturated/Supersaturated Solutions, Solubility Rules, Conductivity, Intermolecular Forces

questions.question: Which of the following is not a net ionic equation?
questions.explanation: NaвЃғ(aq) + ClвЃ»(aq) в†’ NaCl(aq) is not a net ionic equation because NaCl is soluble and remains as ions in solution. This equation doesn't represent a chemical reaction at all. The other options show actual precipitation or neutralization reactions with spectator ions removed.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a net ionic equation?
questions.explanation: Zn(s) + CuClв‚‚(aq) в†’ ZnClв‚‚(aq) + Cu(s) is a full molecular equation, not a net ionic equation. It includes spectator ions (ClвЃ») that should be canceled out in a net ionic equation.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a net ionic equation?
questions.explanation: AgNOв‚ѓ(aq) + NaCl(aq) в†’ AgCl(s) + NaNOв‚ѓ(aq) is a full molecular equation. The net ionic equation would be AgвЃғ(aq) + ClвЃ»(aq) в†’ AgCl(s), with NOв‚ѓвЃ» and NaвЃғ as spectator ions.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a net ionic equation?
questions.explanation: 2KвЃғ(aq) + 2ClвЃ»(aq) + PbВІвЃғ(aq) + 2NOв‚ѓвЃ»(aq) в†’ PbClв‚‚(s) + 2KвЃғ(aq) + 2NOв‚ѓвЃ»(aq) includes spectator ions (KвЃғ and NOв‚ѓвЃ») on both sides. The net ionic equation should be PbВІвЃғ(aq) + 2ClвЃ»(aq) в†’ PbClв‚‚(s).

questions.question: Which of the following is not a net ionic equation?
questions.explanation: HCl(aq) + NaOH(aq) в†’ NaCl(aq) + Hв‚‚O(l) is a full molecular equation. The net ionic equation would be HвЃғ(aq) + OHвЃ»(aq) в†’ Hв‚‚O(l), with NaвЃғ and ClвЃ» as spectator ions.

questions.question: A solution is saturated at room temperature, 25В°C. Afterwards, it is cooled to 20В°C with no change to the appearance of the liquid. How would we describe this solution?
questions.explanation: When a saturated solution is cooled and no precipitate forms, it contains more solute than normally possible at the lower temperature. This is called a supersaturated solution.

questions.question: A saturated solution of KNOв‚ѓ at 20В°C contains 32 g of solute in 100 g of water. If a student dissolves 15 g of KNOв‚ѓ in 50 g of water at the same temperature, how is the solution classified?
questions.explanation: At saturation, 50 g of water can dissolve 16 g of KNOв‚ѓ (half of 32 g). Since only 15 g is dissolved, the solution is unsaturated.

questions.question: The molar solubility of Silver Chloride (AgCl) is $1.33 \times 10^{-5}\text{ M}$ at $25\text{В°C}$. Calculate the $K_{sp}$ for $AgCl$.
questions.explanation:
AgCl dissociates as: AgCl(s) в‡Њ AgвЃғ(aq) + ClвЃ»(aq)
Ksp = [AgвЃғ][ClвЃ»] = s Г— s = sВІ
Ksp = (1.33 Г— 10вЃ»вЃµ)ВІ = 1.77 Г— 10вЃ»В№вЃ°

questions.question: The molar solubility of Calcium Fluoride (CaFв‚‚) is $2.1 \times 10^{-4}\text{ M}$ at a specific temperature. Calculate the $K_{sp}$.
questions.explanation:
CaFв‚‚ dissociates as: CaFв‚‚(s) в‡Њ CaВІвЃғ(aq) + 2FвЃ»(aq)
Ksp = [CaВІвЃғ][FвЃ»]ВІ = (s)(2s)ВІ = 4sВі
Ksp = 4 Г— (2.1 Г— 10вЃ»вЃө)Ві = 4 Г— 9.261 Г— 10вЃ»В№ВІ = 3.70 Г— 10вЃ»В№В№

questions.question: The solubility of NaCl is 36.0 g per 100 mL of water. A solution is prepared by adding 9.0 g of NaCl to 25 mL of water. Classify the solution.
questions.explanation: For 25 mL of water, the maximum amount of NaCl that can dissolve is (36.0 g/100 mL) Г— 25 mL = 9.0 g. Exactly 9.0 g is added, so the solution is saturated.

questions.question: Which of the following tests can be used to distinguish between an ionic solution and most molecular solutions?
questions.explanation: Ionic solutions conduct electricity due to the presence of mobile ions, while most molecular solutions do not conduct electricity well. Conductivity tests can distinguish between them.

questions.question: Which type(s) of molecule(s) are polar solvents more likely to be able to dissolve?
questions.explanation: According to the principle 'like dissolves like', polar solvents are good at dissolving polar and ionic substances. Non-polar substances are not soluble in polar solvents.

questions.question: Which forces affect solubility?
questions.explanation: Solubility is primarily determined by the intermolecular forces between solute and solvent molecules. Intramolecular forces (bonds within molecules) are not broken during dissolution.

questions.question: Which of the following is the least soluble in water?
questions.explanation: Silver acetate is slightly soluble (moderately soluble) in water. Lead(II) nitrate and magnesium sulfide are soluble, and lithium phosphate is soluble because LiвЃғ is a Group 1 ion. Silver acetate has the lowest solubility among these options.

scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on distinguishing between saturated, unsaturated, and supersaturated solutions.
scoreMessages.0: Please review net ionic equations, solubility rules, and solution classification concepts.
